dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3235 IS: Disease Intervention through Nutrition Education Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey (for himself, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Kim, and Mrs. Gillibrand) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To amend the Older Americans Act of 1965 to provide for food-based interventions.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Disease Intervention through Nutrition Education Act or the DINE Act.

subsection.header: Definition of disease prevention and health promotion services
subsection.text: Section 102(14) of the Older Americans Act of 1965 (42 U.S.C. 3002(14)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (B), by inserting screening for eligibility for Federal and non-Federal (including community-based) food is medicine programs and before routine;
in subparagraph (C), by inserting , including referrals to Federal and non-Federal (including community-based) food is medicine programs, if appropriate after caregivers; and
in subparagraph (D), by inserting (including from medically tailored meals and produce prescriptions) after improved nutrition.

subsection.text: Section 205(a)(2)(A) of the Older Americans Act of 1965 (42 U.S.C. 3016(a)(2)(A)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (vi), by inserting , including the use of innovative approaches, including food-based interventions, such as produce prescriptions after systems; and
in clause (viii), by inserting and innovative approaches, including intervention programs, such as produce prescriptions, after including strategies.

subsection.text: Section 214(2)(C) of the Older Americans Act of 1965 (42 U.S.C. 3020e(2)(C)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting interventions and after other; and
by inserting and referral to food-based intervention programs after and counseling.
